LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT CARDIOVASCULAR RESEARCH FOUNDATION'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT - DEFENDANT CARDIOVASCULAR RESEARCH FOUNDATION'S DEMURRER TO THE FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION IN THE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITH TWENTY DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND TO ALLEGE, IF PLAINTIFF LOURDES ZAMUDIO, CAN DO SO IN GOOD FAITH SUFFICIENT ULTIMATE FACTS SHOWING THAT THE ASSAULT COMMITTED BY MR. DAMHUIS AGAINST MS. ZAMUDIO IS THE CONDUCT OF CRF FOR PURPOSES OF THE 2007 AGREEMENT. SEE MINUTES FOR DETAILS. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; REPORTER: IRENE TOFFT, CSR # 12313 (302/JRM)

MINI MINUTES FOR AUG-10-2017 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT CARDIOVASCULAR RESEARCH FOUNDATION'S, DEMURRER TO SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT, AS TO THE SOLE CAUSE OF ACTION FOR BREACH OF WRITTEN CONTRACT ALLEGED IN THE COMPLAINT FILED BY PLAINTIFF LOURDES ZAMUDIO IS OVERRULED. MS. ZAMUDIO'S CLAIM ALLEGES THAT, PER THE INDEMNIFICATION LANGUAGE IN PARAGRAPH 11 OF 2007 AGREEMENT, CRF IS REQUIRED TO PAY THE DAMAGES SUFFERED BY MS. ZAMUDIO, MARRIOTT'S EMPLOYEE, AS A RESULT OF THE ASSAULT COMMITTED AGAINST HER BY ALEXANDER DAMHUIS, CRF'S EMPLOYEE, IN ONE OF THE ROOMS RENTED BY CRF PER THE AGREEMENT. THE 2007 AGREEMENT IS REASONABLY SUSCEPTIBLE TO THE INTERPRETATION PROFFERED BY MS. ZAMUDIO THAT IN THESE CIRCUMSTANCES SHE HAS A CLAIM AGAINST CRF. THE SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT MATERIALLY DIFFERS FROM THE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT IN THAT THE FORMER EXPRESSLY ALLEGES THAT THE ASSAULT OCCURRED IN ONE OF THE ROOMS THAT IS THE SUBJECT OF THE CONTRACT. FAIRLY READ, PARAGRAPH 11 OF THE 2007 AGREEMENT COULD COVER WILLFUL MISCONDUCT COMMITTED BY THE EMPLOYEE OF ONE PARTY TO THAT AGREEMENT IN ONE OF THE ROOMS COVERED BY THE AGREEMENT WHICH CAUSES HARM TO AN EMPLOYEE OF THE OTHER PARTY TO THE AGREEMENT. THE COURT PREVIOUSLY RULED THAT PARAGRAPH 11 IS REASONABLY SUSCEPTIBLE TO THE INTERPRETATION PROFFERED BY MS. ZAMUDIO THAT IT IS NOT LIMITED TO THIRD PARTY CLAIMS, BUT COVERS DIRECT OR FIRST PARTY CLAIMS, AND THAT RULING IS BINDING ON THIS DEMURRER. WRITTEN ORDER IS SIGNED AND FILED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: J. RIOS MERIDA; REPORTER: GORDON F. AIAVAO, CSR NO. 11216, EMAIL: GAIAVAO@SFTC.ORG. (D302)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Against Lourdes Zamudio

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Aug-04-2017 Continued To Oct-04-17 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Aug-10-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, Loren E. Schwartz of the law offices of Dunn & Panagotacos LLP (415-982-2100) appears as counsel for plaintiff/opposing party

Attorney, Kimberly L. Moore of the law offices of Poole Shaffery Law Firm (925-627-3205) appears as counsel for defendant/moving party

The cause is argued and submitted by both sides as set forth in the transcript of the certified shorthand reporter.

Court having reviewed the pleading and having heard oral argument, tentative ruling is confirmed as follows:

DEFENDANT CARDIOVASCULAR RESEARCH FOUNDATION's, DEMURRER TO SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT, as to the sole cause of action for breach of written contract alleged in the complaint filed by plaintiff Lourdes Zamudio is OVERRULED. Ms. Zamudio's claim alleges that, per the indemnification language in paragraph 11 of 2007 agreement, CRF is required to pay the damages suffered by Ms. Zamudio, Marriott's employee, as a result of the assault committed against her by Alexander Damhuis, CRF's employee, in one of the rooms rented by CRF per the agreement. The 2007 agreement is reasonably susceptible to the interpretation proffered by Ms. Zamudio that in these circumstances she has a claim against CRF. The second amended complaint materially differs from the first amended complaint in that the former expressly alleges that the assault occurred in one of the rooms that is the subject of the contract. Fairly read, paragraph 11 of the 2007 agreement could cover willful misconduct committed by the employee of one party to that agreement in one of the rooms covered by the agreement which causes harm to an employee of the other party to the agreement. The court previously ruled that paragraph 11 is reasonably susceptible to the interpretation proffered by Ms. Zamudio that it is not limited to third party claims, but covers direct or first party claims, and that ruling is binding on this demurrer. Written order is signed and filed in open court.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: J. Rios Merida; Reporter: Gordon F. Aiavao, CSR No. 11216, email: gaiavao@sftc.org. (D302)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jun-02-2017 Continued To Aug-23-17 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar May-30-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. H. NELSON MEEKS, TEL. NO. 1-415-989-9915 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. KIMBERLY L. MOORE OF POOLE & SHAFFERY, LLP, TEL. NO. 1-925-627-3200 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT CARDIOVASCULAR RESEARCH FOUNDATION'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT - Defendant Cardiovascular Research Foundation's demurrer to the first cause of action in the first amended complaint is sustained with twenty days leave to amend to allege, if plaintiff Lourdes Zamudio, can do so in good faith sufficient ultimate facts showing that the assault committed by Mr. Damhuis against Ms. Zamudio is the conduct of CRF for purposes of the 2007 agreement. Simply alleging that Mr. Damhuis was employed by CRF is insufficient since there is no indication in the agreement that the conduct of CRF's employee is always the conduct of CRF nor is the agreement reasonably susceptible to such an interpretation. While it may be true that most indemnification agreements cover only third party claims, that is not always true. As shown by Zalkind v. Ceradyne, Inc. (2011) 194 Cal. App. 4th 1010, 1023-29 and cases cited therein, there are numerous examples in California case law where direct or first party claims were held to be covered by indemnification agreements. The indemnification language in the 2007 agreement is ambiguous on this point and that language is reasonably susceptible to Ms. Zamudio's implied interpretation of that language in the FAC as covering a claim she has against CRF. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Irene Tofft, CSR # 12313 =302/hk
